name: Camino del Socorro Trail
name_original: Ermita del Socorro y Sagrado Corazón de Jesús — circular desde Sacedón
description: Hike the moderate 8.7-mile Camino del Socorro Trail in Sacedón, featuring 1438 feet of elevation gain and scenic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Sacedón offers diverse terrain, characterized by the Guadiela River, rolling hills, and areas of Mediterranean forest. The region features a mix of open landscapes and paths leading to local landmarks. Hikers can explore trails that follow river courses or ascend gentle slopes, providing varied perspectives of the area. Best hiking trails around Sacedón The most popular hiking route is Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47), a 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate circular path follows the Guadiela River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino del Socorro Trail, a moderate 8.7 miles (13.9 km) path. This trail traverses varied terrain, including open fields and forested sections. Local hikers also love the Auñón to the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through rural landscapes to a sanctuary, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Sacedón is defined by river valleys, gentle hills, and local points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Sacedón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 35 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Sacedón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sacedón?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Sacedón, offering a variety of experiences. These include 6 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 1 more challenging option, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Sacedón?
A: Yes, Sacedón has 6 easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt;, which is short and features minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Sacedón's hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Sacedón is diverse, characterized by the Guadiela River, rolling hills, and Mediterranean forests. You'll find a mix of open landscapes, paths following river courses, and gentle slopes, providing varied perspectives of the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sacedón?
A: Yes, Sacedón offers several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012673371/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-los-desamparados-circular-along-the-guadiela-river-pr-cu-47" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47)&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a scenic loop along the river.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer access to local points of interest. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt;, or reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4313970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features impressive viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the surrounding reservoirs.

Q: Are there any hikes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the Sacedón region is known for its proximity to large reservoirs. Some trails offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3764775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;, providing opportunities to enjoy the expansive water landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sacedón?
A: The hiking routes in Sacedón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 35 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain and the well-maintained paths that lead to local points of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sacedón?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Sacedón, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are typically mild but can be chilly.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Sacedón suitable for families?
A: Many of the easy and moderate trails around Sacedón are suitable for families. Routes with shorter distances and less elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop&lt;/a&gt;, are good options for outings with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Sacedón?
A: Most natural trails in the Sacedón area are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry water for them. Please check for any specific local regulations or protected areas.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in Sacedón?
A: Parking is generally available near the starting points of many popular trails in Sacedón. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the individual tour descriptions on komoot, as they often provide details on parking facilities.

Q: How long do hikes around Sacedón typically take?
A: Hikes around Sacedón vary in duration. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under an hour, while moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025793863/aunon-to-the-sanctuary-of-the-virgen-del-madronal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Auñón to the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 to 4 hours.
tips: The hermitage of the Virgen del Socorro is located about six kilometers from the town, in the middle of a landscape of dense forests and clean heights, overlooking the depth of the Tagus river, pooled in the Bolarque reservoir and in front of the impressive rock of the Castle of Anguix. This enclave has, in addition to the devotional and historical value that it contains, the characteristic of being an extraordinary viewpoint of the Tagus valley, and on the path that takes us to its height you can go contemplating the grandeur of the Alcarria that collects in the large reservoirs from Entrepeñas and Buendía the waters of the Tagus and Guadiela.
The hill of La Coronilla offers us an impressive view and the Monument to the Heart of Jesus.
Panoramic views of the Entrepeñas reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The Camino del Socorro Trail is a moderate hike that generally takes around 4 hours and 11 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how long you spend enjoying the viewpoints and highlights along the way.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', the trail involves some elevation gain (around 438 meters up). While not overly strenuous, beginners should be prepared for a sustained walk and some uphill sections. It's a good option for those looking to step up from easier walks.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Camino del Socorro Trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular hike, the terrain will vary. Expect a mix of paths and tracks, typical for a rural Spanish hike. Given the elevation changes, there will be ascents and descents, so sturdy footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the historic Hermitage of Santa María del Socorro and the impressive Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus of La Coronilla. Additionally, you'll be treated to scenic Views of the Entrepeñas Reservoir.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Sacedón. While specific parking details aren't provided, Sacedón is a town, so street parking or designated parking areas are generally available near the start of popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Camino del Socorro Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially near religious sites or if passing through any agricultural areas, to ensure their safety and respect for wildlife and other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, and there are no entrance fees to hike the Camino del Socorro Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to hike in this region of Spain. The weather is usually milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable hiking and better enjoyment of the scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Sacedón, you'll find various amenities in the town, such as cafes, restaurants, and possibly accommodation options. It's a good idea to stock up on water and snacks before you begin your hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 48% of this route follows sections of the official 'Camino del Socorro' path.
